question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set an arbitrary `X-Real-IP` header value to inject a spoofed IP address into the trace log record, causing mirror node audit logs to contain incorrect client identity records that misattribute requests to a different IP?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set the `X-Forwarded-For` header to an arbitrary string (e.g., `10.0.0.1, admin, internal`) so that the full unsanitized string is logged as the client IP, causing incorrect and misleading records to be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set both `X-Real-IP` and `X-Forwarded-For` headers simultaneously, causing `getClientIpAddress()` to always prefer `X-Real-IP` and silently discard the `X-Forwarded-For` value, resulting in incomplete or misleading IP attribution in exported mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an `X-Real-IP` header containing newline characters (e.g., `\\n`) to inject additional fake log lines into the trace output, causing mirror nodes to receive fabricated request records that never actually occurred?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an `X-Forwarded-For` header containing newline characters or CRLF sequences to perform log injection, causing the mirror node to export records for phantom requests that were never processed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Real-IP` to an empty string to force fallback to `X-Forwarded-For`, and then set `X-Forwarded-For` to a spoofed value, causing the mirror node trace record to log an incorrect originating IP address?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a malformed `RemoteAddr` (e.g., no port component) causing `net.SplitHostPort()` to fail silently and return an empty IP string, resulting in a trace record exported to mirror nodes with a blank client IP field?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Real-IP` to a value containing format string specifiers (e.g., `%s`, `%d`) that, when passed to `fmt.Sprintf()` in `TracingMiddleware()`, produce a malformed or truncated log record exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request URL path containing newline characters via `request.URL.RequestURI()` to inject fake log entries into the trace output, causing mirror nodes to receive records for requests that never occurred?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a path that exactly matches one of the `internalPaths` keys (e.g., `/metrics`, `/health/liveness`, `/health/readiness`) to cause their request to be logged at `log.Debug` level instead of `log.Info`, resulting in the request record being missing from mirror node exports when debug logging is disabled?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to `/metrics` (an `internalPaths` entry) causing the trace record to be emitted only at debug level, so that if the mirror node log aggregator is configured at info level or above, the record is silently dropped and never exported?

[File: 'FileName: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to `/health/liveness` causing the trace to be logged at `log.Debug` level, resulting in the access record being absent from mirror node exports when the production log level is set to info or higher?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to `/health/readiness` to exploit the `internalPaths` debug-level suppression, causing the request to be invisible in mirror node exported records even though the endpoint was accessed and responded?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a query string containing sensitive data (e.g., `?token=secret`) which is captured by `request.URL.RequestURI()` and logged verbatim at `log.Info` level, causing sensitive parameters to be incorrectly included in mirror node exported records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Real-IP` to a non-IP string (e.g., `attacker-hostname` or `localhost`) causing a non-IP value to be logged as the client address in mirror node trace records, making IP-based audit trails incorrect?
